title: Order shared memory with the correct barrier
description: Use the minimal correct memory fence / atomic ordering for cross-thread visibility instead of relying on volatile or accidental ordering.
body: Use when threads communicate through shared memory and correctness depends on ordering of reads/writes. Do not use volatile as a synchronization primitive and do not assume sequential consistency on weakly-ordered ISAs (ARM). Actions: choose acquire on the consumer, release on the producer, or seq_cst where a total order is required; document why each fence exists, and test under a thread sanitizer / stress harness. Validation signals include a clean TSan run and a stress test that does not reorder-fail across thousands of iterations on the weakest target. Risk boundary: a missing or too-weak fence is a heisenbug that appears only on certain CPUs under load; treat any unexplained ordering as blocking and escalate for review.
